title: Implement wishlist workflows and product projection
verify: Add validates exists plus published plus current-sales-channel plus active-category visibility and converges under duplicate/concurrent calls.
Remove filters by customer_id plus product_id and is idempotent when absent.
List and add return the same exact minimal WishlistItem and WishlistProductProjection contract.
Missing, unpublished, channel-invisible, or inactive-category products are omitted without deletion; out-of-stock remains visible with is_available false.
evidence_required: Exact projection, four hidden cases, and out-of-stock visibility assertions.
Backend typecheck and Memory Bank lint output.
constraints: Use Wishlist Module for records and supported Product/query boundary for current FT-001/FT-002 truth.
Return only the exact WishlistProductProjection; do not copy CatalogProduct or ProductDetail.
Write workflows take a server-supplied customer ID; they do not authenticate requests themselves.
No product lifecycle hooks, cleanup worker, event, or cached product snapshot.
invariants: Mutation lookup always includes customer_id and product_id.
Duplicate add yields one row and the same item shape used by list.
Four hidden visibility cases are omitted while out-of-stock remains visible/unavailable.
Hidden/missing product never exposes stale snapshot or fails the full list.
purpose: Implement deterministic wishlist domain behavior between persistence and authenticated HTTP routes.
success_outcome: Workflows safely add/remove one customer product and return the exact current minimal projection under retries, concurrency, visibility, and stock changes.
anti_goals: Do not expose Store routes or authenticate users.
Do not cache product snapshots or add cleanup jobs.

stop_conditions: Product publication, sales-channel, active-category, price, or availability cannot be resolved through supported query boundaries.
Concurrent idempotency would require a distributed lock instead of database uniqueness.
Exact projection would need a stored product snapshot or full catalog/detail copy.
